task_completion.description: Verify that a synthetic session has been started in the OpenBCI GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenBCI GUI is open and the data source is configured for a 'Synthetic' session (often visible in the session setup screen or system control panel).

task_completion.description: Verify that the data stream is actively running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Stop Data Stream' button is visible (indicating the stream is currently active), and time-series data or graphs are actively rendering on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Band Power' widget is open and visible in the GUI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A widget panel with the exact title 'Band Power' is displayed within the OpenBCI GUI layout.

task_completion.description: Verify that the settings or configuration menu for the Band Power widget has been accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The settings dropdown, gear icon menu, or configuration panel specific to the Band Power widget is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that only the Alpha band is enabled, while Delta, Theta, Beta, and Gamma are disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Within the Band Power widget or its settings, only the 'Alpha' band is active or visible. Delta, Theta, Beta, and Gamma should appear unchecked, hidden, or explicitly disabled. (Note: If all 5 bands are still visible and cannot be hidden, this item should not receive points).

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the standard OpenBCI GUI graphical interface, not a terminal, script output, or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not attempt to rewrite the application source code to bypass the missing feature.
integrity.visual_evidence: No code editors (like VS Code, Processing IDE, or terminal text editors) are open modifying .pde files (e.g., W_BandPower.pde).

integrity.description: Verify the agent didn't just type the expected outcome into a text field or terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows or text editors where the agent simply printed 'Alpha band isolated' or similar text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified facts: 1) The widget title is 'Band Power'. 2) 'Alpha' is one of the five hardcoded bands displayed in the widget. 3) The source code (W_BandPower.pde) lacks any feature, setting, or checkboxes to hide specific bands or isolate a single band. The task instructions to 'uncheck' Delta, Theta, Beta, and Gamma refer to a non-existent feature. Score the final isolation step strictly based on visual evidence; if the other bands are still visible, the step is not complete.
